EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong storm system and cold front moved through southern New England on Friday bringing a 4-8 hour period of strong winds which caused sporadic damage in Rhode Island. Winds generally gusted 50-60 mph.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Winds generally gusted 50-60 mph but some sporadic gusts were higher including an 81 mph gust at a New Shoreham CWOP at 11:21 AM EST.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Winds generally gusted 40-50 mph. In Pawtucket trees and powerlines were down at the intersection of Beverage Hill and Prospect St.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong low was centered over the Gulf of Maine on the morning of the 7th creating a tight pressure gradient across southern New England. This led to strong wind gusts generally 45 to 65 mph.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Winds generally gusted 40 to 60 mph. The strongest gust was 61 mph on the Block Island Jetty.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Very hot and humid weather occurred in southern New England over the course of 4 days, peaking on Tuesday June 24th when heat index values reached 105 to 110. All four climate sites easily set daily records, and Providence and Boston set all time June high temperature records. There were reports of several dozen people treated for heat related illnesses, mostly at local beaches. Three recruits at the Massachusetts Firefighting Academy in Stow were hospitalized Wednesday morning after falling ill due to the extreme temperatures. Winds turning off the ocean finally broke the heat by June 26th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The closest climate site at T.F. Green Airport (KPVD) beat both its daily record and it's all time June high temperature record with 100F. Three beachgoers were taken to the hospital with heat related injuries in Narragansett.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front brought a round of mostly sub severe storms to southern New England in the afternoon, but one storm was able to produce some damaging winds in central Rhode Island.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: In Cranston, a tree and wires were down.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front dropped through southern New England in the afternoon and evening causing a line of strong storms to move through CT, RI, and southeast MA causing wind damage in each state. Eastern CT also saw hail from quarter to golf ball sized.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: In Barrington, a tree was down on a vehicle on County Rd.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A mid level shortwave and surface cold front brough a smattering of strong to severe storms to southern New England in the evening and early overnight hours. This resulted in scattered wind damage reports in central/northeast CT, northern RI, and central MA. It also resulted in some flash flooding in southeast MA.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: In Bristol there was a garage fire caused by a lightning strike.
